audit_result.proof_trace: checkUpkeep correctly soft-skips via continue/conditional for unconfigured (decimals==0), low-value, and stale-with-no-fallback assets. However, _getAssetPrice is called with withValidation=false, which suppresses explicit revert guards (L410-415) but NOT exceptions from the external latestRoundData() call at L385-386. When a data stream price is stale AND a fallback AggregatorV3 feed is configured, latestRoundData() is called without try/catch; if that feed reverts (deprecated aggregator, access-controlled feed, circuit breaker), the revert propagates through the full checkUpkeep call, violating the no-revert soft-skip invariant.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Asset A's data stream price becomes stale; the configured fallback AggregatorV3 feed is deprecated or paused and reverts on latestRoundData(); the revert bubbles up through _getAssetPrice → checkUpkeep, causing Chainlink Automation to receive a revert on every checkUpkeep call, blocking all auction starts and auction-end processing until the feed is fixed.

PROP-01b-partial3-inv-036.text: checkUpkeep uses soft-skip (no revert) for ineligible/stale/low-value assets.
PROP-01b-partial3-inv-036.assertion: forall checkUpkeep(asset): ineligible condition => continue (no revert), return false
